mission.title: Install and Configure LocalAI
mission.description: LocalAI is an open-source alternative to proprietary AI models, designed for self-hosting on consumer-grade hardware. It supports various AI tasks including text generation, audio, video, and image processing without requiring a GPU. Ideal for users seeking a decentralized and local-first AI solution.

steps.title: Install the LocalAI CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the LocalAI command-line interface which is necessary for managing LocalAI deployments.

```bash

curl -Lo localai https://github.com/go-skynet/LocalAI/releases/download/v3.12.1/localai-linux-amd64

chmod +x localai

sudo mv localai /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the LocalAI binary for Linux.
> Make the binary executable.
> Move the binary to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Kubernetes cluster using Minikube, which is suitable for local development and testing.

```bash

minikube start --kubernetes-version=v1.30 --cpus=2 --memory=4096

```

> Start Minikube with specified resources.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig to interact with the Minikube cluster.

```bash

kubectl config use-context minikube

```

> Switch to the Minikube context.

steps.title: Deploy LocalAI on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This step deploys LocalAI to your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add localai https://charts.localai.io

helm install localai localai/localai --version 3.12.1 --namespace localai --create-namespace

```

> Add the LocalAI Helm repository.
> Install LocalAI using Helm.

steps.title: Verify the deployment
steps.description:
This step checks if the LocalAI pods are running successfully.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n localai

```

> List the pods in the LocalAI namespace to verify deployment.

uninstall.title: Uninstall LocalAI
uninstall.description:
This step removes LocalAI from your Kubernetes cluster and warns about potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall localai --namespace localai

kubectl delete namespace localai

```

> Uninstall the LocalAI release.
> Delete the LocalAI namespace, which may lead to data loss.

upgrade.title: Upgrade LocalAI
upgrade.description:
Backup your current deployment, upgrade to the latest version, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl cp localai-pod:/path/to/data ./backup-data

helm upgrade localai localai/localai --version 3.12.1 --namespace localai

kubectl get pods -n localai

```

> Backup your data from the LocalAI pod.
> Upgrade LocalAI to the latest version.
> Verify that the upgraded pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The LocalAI image may not be available or the repository URL is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Check the image repository URL and ensure the image exists.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The LocalAI application may be misconfigured or missing required resources.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n localai' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes API server may be overloaded or unreachable.

**Fix:**
Check your network connectivity and the status of the Kubernetes cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create pod sandbox'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient resources allocated to the Kubernetes cluster.

**Fix:**
Increase the CPU and memory limits for your Minikube cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new AI models and improved API performance.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 3.11.x, ensure to update your model configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see the LocalAI pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n localai'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
